# Recommended Briefing Agenda: Sage Cloudberry

## WonderPaws Pet Wellness | Full-Day Executive Briefing (First Ever)

---

**Visitor:** Sage Cloudberry, CIO, WonderPaws Pet Wellness
**Additional Attendee:** "Kubernetes" (therapy golden retriever, registered service animal)
**VVIP Tier:** Standard (first briefing)
**Date:** TBD (pending scheduling)
**Location:** Raleigh Executive Briefing Center -- Pet-Friendly Collaboration Room
**Executive Sponsor:** TBD (to be assigned post-briefing)
**Briefing Coordinator:** EBC Operations Team

---

### Agenda Overview

This is WonderPaws's first executive briefing with Red Hat. Sage is a former SRE evaluating OpenShift vs. VMware Tanzu for a $850K initial investment, with $3M+ growth potential over 3 years. The entire day is designed to feel like a technical working session between peers -- not a vendor pitch. Sage will want hands-on time to deploy something live. Open-source community story is emphasized throughout. The briefing closes with sponsor assignment discussion, as WonderPaws currently has no assigned sponsor. Pet-friendly accommodations are required throughout (Kubernetes the golden retriever will attend).

---

| Time | Duration | Session | Lead |

### Session Details

#### 9:00 AM -- Welcome with Matcha + Vegan Breakfast (30 min)

**Format:** Casual welcome in Pet-Friendly Collaboration Room
**Protocol:** Standard VVIP (first briefing) -- EBC Manager greets at lobby
**Catering:** Vegan breakfast spread (avocado toast, acai bowls, fresh fruit, vegan pastries), matcha latte service, oat milk coffee, sparkling water
**Setup:** Casual seating, dog bed and water bowl for Kubernetes, whiteboards, multiple monitors for demos

**Talking points for welcome:**
- Warmly welcome both Sage and Kubernetes by name
- Light conversation: WonderPaws growth, Portland, her Medium blog
- Set expectations: "Today is a technical working session. We want to show you everything, including the things that don't work yet."

> **Critical:** Greet Kubernetes (the dog) by name. Sage will appreciate it and it sets the tone for the day.

---

#### 9:30 AM -- WonderPaws Context Discussion (60 min)

**Format:** Discovery-style conversation led by Sage
**Presenter:** Account Team (mostly listening)
**Objective:** Let Sage talk about WonderPaws's growth, her platform challenges, and her vision. We need to deeply understand the context before presenting anything.

**Discussion prompts (but let Sage drive):**
- WonderPaws's 40% YoY growth and what that means operationally
- Her first few months as the first CIO hire
- Current platform state (mixed OpenShift + VMware Tanzu)
- Her vision for the next 2-3 years
- What she wants to get out of this briefing

> **Note:** Do not present anything yet. This session is about listening. Take detailed notes. Reference Sage's specific challenges in later sessions.

---

#### 10:30 AM -- Red Hat Platform Overview (Technical) (90 min)

**Format:** Technical architecture walkthrough (NOT marketing)
**Presenter:** Platform Engineering Team
**Priority:** HIGH -- sets the technical credibility baseline

**Objective:** Present the OpenShift platform with technical depth appropriate for a former SRE. Include honest comparisons to VMware Tanzu where relevant.

**Content to cover:**
- OpenShift architecture (from kernel to platform services)
- Operator framework and how it differs from Tanzu approach
- Upgrade path and operational model
- Honest comparison: OpenShift strengths vs. Tanzu strengths
- Cost model discussion
- Open-source upstream components and community governance

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Bring engineering (not product marketing) to present
- [ ] Prepare honest OpenShift vs. Tanzu comparison
- [ ] Have architecture diagrams at whiteboard
- [ ] Be ready for deep technical questions from an SRE

> **Hard rule:** This is not a marketing pitch. If a presenter uses phrases like "industry-leading" or "best-in-class," redirect them. Sage will check out.

---

#### 12:00 PM -- Vegan Working Lunch (60 min)

**Format:** Informal working lunch
**Catering:** Vegan lunch spread (grain bowls, Buddha bowls, fresh salads, vegan desserts, matcha service)
**Presenter:** Account Team + continuing architecture discussion

**Objective:** Continue the morning conversation informally. Not a separate presentation.

**Content to cover:**
- Follow-up questions from morning sessions
- Sage's personal path from SRE to CIO
- Discussion of the afternoon lab (what she wants to deploy)

> **Note:** Ensure vegan options are thoughtful and substantive. Not just a sad salad. Sage has blogged about frustration with token vegan options at conferences.

---

#### 1:00 PM -- Hands-on Lab: OpenShift Deployment (120 min)

**Format:** Interactive hands-on lab -- Sage drives
**Facilitator:** Senior Solutions Architect
**Setup:** Pre-provisioned lab environment, Sage's laptop connected, backup cluster ready
**Priority:** CRITICAL -- this is the make-or-break session

**Objective:** Sage will want to deploy something live. Give her full access to a real cluster and let her actually work. This session has the highest impact on the platform decision.

**Lab exercises:**
1. Bootstrap OpenShift cluster on demo infrastructure (20 min)
2. Deploy a sample veterinary EMR-like workload (25 min)
3. Configure autoscaling for growth scenarios (20 min)
4. Deploy and test a small ML model (veterinary imaging classifier) (30 min)
5. Explore the operator framework (15 min)
6. Open exploration -- Sage can break or build anything (30 min)

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Lab environment pre-provisioned and tested
- [ ] Sample workloads relevant to veterinary use case
- [ ] Solutions Architect who can handle deep technical questions
- [ ] Backup cluster in case primary breaks
- [ ] GitHub repo with all sample code Sage can take with her

> **Note:** Sage will probably want to contribute a fix or improvement to something during this session. Be ready to guide her to the right upstream project.

---

#### 3:00 PM -- Veterinary AI Use Case Workshop (60 min)

**Format:** Working session on AI for veterinary use cases
**Presenter:** AI Product Team + Healthcare Solutions Architect
**Tie to pipeline:** Supports the $320K Veterinary AI Platform opportunity

**Objective:** Present OpenShift AI with specific veterinary use cases. Connect to Sage's vision for predictive pet wellness.

**Content to cover:**
- OpenShift AI architecture overview
- Veterinary-relevant ML use cases: imaging classification, EHR analytics, predictive wellness
- Data pipeline for veterinary EMR integration
- Model deployment and lifecycle management
- Honest discussion of what AI can and cannot do for veterinary workflows
- Integration with existing veterinary systems

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Research veterinary-specific ML use cases in advance
- [ ] Prepare sample notebook or demo model relevant to veterinary imaging
- [ ] Have healthcare SME present (even if vertical is human healthcare, principles transfer)

---

#### 4:00 PM -- Community + Open Source Deep-Dive (30 min)

**Format:** Conversation with Red Hat Open Source Office representative
**Presenter:** Open Source Program Office (OSPO) lead
**Objective:** Sage is an open-source contributor. Show her how Red Hat actually engages with upstream communities. This is a strong differentiator vs. VMware Tanzu.

**Content to cover:**
- Red Hat's upstream-first development model
- Community governance in key projects (Kubernetes, Ansible, OpenShift)
- How Red Hat employees contribute to upstream projects as a core job responsibility
- Opportunities for Sage to contribute (including her existing Kubernetes operator)
- Community events and conferences relevant to her

> **Opportunity:** Sage's Kubernetes operator for veterinary EMR integration might be interesting to the broader community. Offer to introduce her to relevant SIG contacts.

---

#### 4:30 PM -- Sponsor Assignment Discussion + Next Steps (30 min)

**Format:** Close-out discussion with account planning
**Presenter:** Account Team + Healthcare Vertical Lead

**Objective:** Because WonderPaws has no assigned sponsor, this session discusses who the best fit would be and establishes next steps. Sage deserves input on who her sponsor will be.

**Discussion topics:**
- Sponsor assignment recommendations (healthcare/life sciences vertical with engineering background)
- Next steps for the platform evaluation decision
- Timeline for the $850K initial investment decision
- SAB membership opportunity (if platform decision goes our way)
- Follow-up cadence

**Close-out checklist:**
- [ ] Confirm sponsor recommendations to be finalized within 48 hours
- [ ] Schedule follow-up technical working session with Solutions Architect
- [ ] Confirm delivery of customized platform comparison document
- [ ] Gift presentation (something for Kubernetes the dog, something for Sage)
- [ ] Send GitHub repo access for all lab materials

**Commitments to confirm:**
1. Sponsor assignment within 48 hours
2. Detailed OpenShift vs. Tanzu comparison document (customized for WonderPaws)
3. Follow-up technical workshop in 2-3 weeks
4. Introduction to relevant Kubernetes SIG contacts for Sage's operator
5. Veterinary AI pilot proposal timeline

| Platform decision advancement | OpenShift becomes the leading candidate |
| Sponsor assignment | Finalized within 48 hours |
| Pipeline advancement | $850K opportunity moves to Proposal stage |
| Relationship stage | Advance from Aware to Favorable |
